referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Hayes, Strosahl, & Wilson (1999). ACT Treatment Manual; APA Division 12.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Manual and guideline sources provide the clearest target course length for ACT. The benchmark range is preferred over narrower trial-specific endpoints because it is more generalizable and more conservative for planning.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Cochrane-style review benchmark cited in comparison sheet (Smith et al. 2018); NICE depression guidance.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Short primary-care trials exist, but the broader review-based range is more reliable for a planning table and more conservative than a single short-course estimate.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Symptom-specific change in intrusion frequency or distress.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Iyadurai et al. (2017) Molecular Psychiatry and related brief imagery-interference protocols.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: There is no stable standardized depression-course benchmark for this intervention. A very brief external protocol range is more defensible than inventing a longer duration from sparse trial rows.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Remission target: EPDS < 10 prior to delivery.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: NICE CG192 / antenatal and postnatal mental health guidance.
decision: Guideline benchmark chosen over single-study endpoint
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The NICE-style antenatal benchmark is more reliable than any one trial endpoint. It reflects target clinical duration, whereas some study rows reflect outcome assessment timing.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Martell, Addis, & Jacobson (2001). Depression in Context: Strategies for Guided Action.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Behavioral activation manuals and guideline summaries provide a clearer planning duration than pooled study endpoints. The longer 10 to 16 week range is also the more conservative assumption.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Richards et al. (2016) COBRA / BA protocol family; BA manuals.
decision: Manual benchmark chosen over digital BA endpoint
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Direct BAT should not inherit the shorter hours from a low-intensity digital BA program. The manualized BAT range is the more appropriate and conservative planning figure.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: APA Clinical Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Depression (2019).
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: This category is too broad for a single-study estimate to be trustworthy. APA-style benchmark timing is more reliable and conservative.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Response: >= 50% reduction in PHQ-9 or equivalent.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: NICE depression in adults step-2 / guided self-help benchmark.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: A single old study can underestimate the practical dose of bibliotherapy. The step-care benchmark gives a more generalizable and conservative duration and time commitment.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: WHO mhGAP Intervention Guide; Rathod et al. (2015).
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Culturally adapted CBT is still brief CBT. The external benchmark is more reliable than treating one study duration as universal.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Beck, Rush, Shaw, & Emery (1979); APA Clinical Practice Guideline.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The pooled study row is highly heterogeneous. Standard CBT manuals and guidelines provide the most defensible planning range, and it is more conservative than short web-based or workshop rows.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Beck et al. (1979); APA Clinical Practice Guideline.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Cognitive therapy is best treated as a full-course weekly therapy in planning terms. The manual benchmark is more reliable than a shorter pooled estimate.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Gilbert (2009). The Compassionate Mind / compassion-focused therapy manual tradition.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Single-study estimates exist, but the manual-based group range better reflects usual delivery and is more conservative on total time.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Rehm (1977). Self-Control Model of Depression.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Because the evidence base is sparse and often group-based, the longer manual-style benchmark is more reliable than a single trial row.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Andrews et al. (2010) cCBT meta-analytic benchmark.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Computer-assisted cognitive therapy is best planned like a structured CBT course with digital support. The benchmark range is more generalizable than one older trial.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Folkman et al. (1991). Coping Effectiveness Training Manual.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The manual gives the clearest fixed course length. This is preferable to sparse depression-specific study rows.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Bower et al. (2011) review benchmark; NICE depression guidance.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Counselling duration varies, so the broad review-based range is more reliable and more conservative than a single weighted row.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Karyotaki et al. (2017) digital depression treatment benchmark; digital BA trial families.
decision: External benchmark chosen over follow-up endpoint
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The 12-week study figure reflects an outcome endpoint after a 6-week program. Because the requested column is target clinical duration, the external 6 to 10 week program range is the better and more conservative planning value.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: No strict universal acute recovery benchmark established.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Perls, Hefferline, & Goodman (1951); experimental-status style references.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The depression evidence base is sparse. A longer exploratory therapy range is safer and more conservative than a short historical trial estimate.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Burlingame et al. (2013) APA Handbook of Group Psychotherapy.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Recalculated study rows support group CBT, but a group-manual benchmark is more reliable for final planning and conservatively captures longer sessions.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Wilfley et al. (2000). Group Interpersonal Psychotherapy Manual.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The study pool is favorable, but the group IPT manual gives the most reliable course structure and conservatively captures higher contact hours.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Chadwick (2006). Person-Based Cognitive Therapy manual tradition.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Because the evidence base is niche and small, the manual-style group benchmark is safer than relying only on a single pilot estimate.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: NICE depression in adults step-2 guidance.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Guided self-help is a stepped-care format, so NICE provides the most reliable duration. The benchmark is slightly more conservative than the exact 7 to 8 week trial rows.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Constantino et al. (2008). Journal of Clinical Psychology.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The intervention is specialized and the strongest benchmark is the longer 16-week format. This is more conservative than shortening it to standard CBT length.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Andersson & Titov (2014) and iBA program benchmarks.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: A single exact trial suggests 5 weeks, but the broader internet BA literature supports 6 to 8 weeks as a safer planning range.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Cuijpers et al. (2013) Cochrane-style iCBT benchmark.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Single-study rows often show 6 weeks, but broader iCBT evidence and service models usually run longer. The guideline/meta-analytic range is more reliable and more conservative.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Farrer et al. (2011) and digital PST literature.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The exact study row is plausible, but the benchmark range is better for planning because it captures variation across internet PST formats.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Andersson & Titov (2014) World Psychiatry style benchmark; digital treatment guidelines.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: As an umbrella category, internet-based treatment should use a broad program benchmark rather than one pooled study estimate. The chosen range is conservative.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Weissman et al. (2014). Interpersonal Counseling Manual.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: IPC is intentionally briefer than full IPT. The manual benchmark is the clearest and most reliable course-length estimate.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Weissman, Markowitz, & Klerman (2000). Comprehensive Guide to IPT.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Although pooled study timing can be closer to 10 to 12 weeks, the classic IPT benchmark is 12 to 16 sessions and is more conservative for planning.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Weissman, Markowitz, & Klerman (2000); APA guideline support.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: As a broad IPT-family label, it should use the standard established IPT time-limited course rather than a narrower pooled estimate.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Bohlmeijer et al. life review / reminiscence meta-analytic benchmark.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The external range matches the better-weighted study summary and is more reliable than any single reminiscence trial.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Wells (2009). Metacognitive Therapy for Anxiety and Depression.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The exact RCT shows a 10-week format, which sits inside the manual benchmark. The benchmark is therefore preferred as the more reliable and slightly more conservative planning value.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Normann & Morina (2018). Clinical Psychology Review.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The review-based 8-session structure matches the exact study row and is more externally defensible.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Relapse prevention / remission follow-up often assessed over months to 1 year.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Segal, Williams, & Teasdale (2002). MBCT manual.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The shorter study-derived hour estimate undercounts standard MBCT-style delivery. The established 8-week program with longer sessions and retreat is the more reliable and more conservative course estimate.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Relapse prevention / remission follow-up often assessed over months to 1 year.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Kuyken et al. (2016) MBCT benchmark; MBCT manuals.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: A 4-week study-style estimate is too short for standard mindfulness-based therapy. The full MBCT-style benchmark is more reliable and much more conservative.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Ishiyama (1990) / Japanese Society for Morita Therapy benchmarks.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The evidence base is limited, so the broader external benchmark is preferable to one pilot trial.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Remission target often assessed as EPDS < 10 at 12 weeks postpartum.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Munoz et al. (2007). Mothers and Babies Course Instructor's Manual.
decision: Manual benchmark chosen over postpartum endpoint
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The 12-week figure in the study row is an outcome endpoint, not the course length. Because the requested column is target clinical duration, the 6-week manualized course is the correct and more reliable planning value.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Readiness for change / engagement benchmark rather than full remission.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Miller & Rollnick (2012). Motivational Interviewing, 3rd ed.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: MI is commonly brief and adjunctive. The manual benchmark is more appropriate than stretching it into a full depression-treatment duration.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Cuijpers et al. (2012) supportive therapy review; APA guideline support.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The pooled study figure is useful, but a broad supportive-therapy benchmark is more reliable and more conservative for planning.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Andrews et al. (2010) online treatment benchmark.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Because online therapy is a delivery format rather than one therapy, the broader benchmark is more reliable than a thin two-row study synthesis.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Pfeiffer et al. meta-analytic benchmark / peer-CBT literature.
decision: Benchmark chosen with close study support
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The exact Amani-style study row is strong, but the benchmark range is consistent and slightly more conservative on hours.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Lieberman et al. / child-parent psychotherapy manual tradition.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The pilot study is weak for planning. The dyadic psychotherapy manual-style duration is more reliable and more conservative.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Nezu, Nezu, & D'Zurilla (2013). PST manual.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: The pooled study row points to a short format, but the broader PST benchmark is more reliable and conservative.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Shedler (2010); Leichsenring et al.; psychodynamic guideline literature.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Psychodynamic psychotherapy varies widely, but the longer benchmark is more faithful to established practice and more conservative than a short pooled endpoint.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: APA guideline support; STPP/STDP reviews.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: As a family-level category, psychodynamic therapy should use the conservative established range rather than a narrow pooled estimate.

establishedClinicalBenchmark: Response typically assessed at around week 8 or later depending on modality.
referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: APA Clinical Practice Guideline for the Treatment of Depression (2019).
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: This is an umbrella label, so the APA benchmark is more reliable than any pooled estimate from mixed modalities.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: D'Zurilla & Nezu (1999). Social Problem-Solving Therapy Manual.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Older exact-match studies exist, but the manual benchmark is more reliable and more conservative.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: WHO mhGAP Intervention Guide (2016).
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Task-shifting IPC is a low-intensity format, so mhGAP provides the strongest practical benchmark. It is more generalizable than one small pragmatic trial.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Mohr et al. tele-CBT protocol and telepsychotherapy literature.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Telephone psychotherapy is a delivery format, so the tele-CBT protocol range is more reliable than a pooled average over heterogeneous telephone interventions.

referenceGuidelinesAndManuals: Hunot et al. Cochrane-style benchmark; ACT/MBCT literature.
finalRationaleAndCalculationEvidence: Because this is a broad family-level row, the external benchmark is more reliable than any single pooled effect row and more conservative on time.
